description: Shared small-model profile registry for cli-opencode dispatch. Each model declares one or more executor paths with their provider + quota_pool, so the fallback engine can route across pools when one is exhausted. Active small-model rotation: DeepSeek-v4-pro, Kimi-k2.7-code (Kimi For Coding plan via provider `kimi-for-coding`), MiniMax-M3 (default via the MiniMax Token Plan provider `minimax-coding-plan`; pay-per-token Direct API via provider `minimax`), MiMo-V2.5-Pro (via the Xiaomi Token Plan Europe provider `xiaomi-token-plan-ams` or Xiaomi Direct API provider `xiaomi`), GLM-5.2 (Z.AI GLM Coding Plan via provider `zai-coding-plan`). Optional unverified separate-pool stub: Claude Haiku.

executors.notes: Direct DeepSeek API key (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY`). Requires `--pure` flag; rejects tool names with `:`.

strengths: complex reasoning
root-cause debugging
multi-step implementation
architecture trade-off analysis
weaknesses: slower on non-trivial fixtures
needs timeout headroom

visual/illustration tasks: richest individual drawings but gold-heavy and brand-blue-starved despite a brand brief (informal bento bake-off, n=1 fixture, 2026-06-22; see references/models/deepseek-v4-pro.md)

executors.notes: Kimi For Coding plan (subscription) via opencode provider `kimi-for-coding`. Live slug `kimi-for-coding/k2p7` (display name 'Kimi K2.7 Code'); confirmed via `opencode models kimi-for-coding` 2026-06-15 → k2p5/k2p6/k2p7/kimi-k2-thinking, all 262,144-token context / 32,768 output. Coding-optimized Kimi K2.7; dispatch cost 0 (subscription). Omit `--agent` at top level (opencode rejects named agents there). `--variant high` is accepted (exit 0, smoke-tested 2026-06-15) though its effect is benchmark-unverified. Re-verify the live slug before dispatch — Kimi/Moonshot ids drift; do NOT use the invented `kimi-token-plan-ams` slug.

capability.notes: Live slug `kimi-for-coding/k2p7` confirmed responsive 2026-06-15 (`opencode models kimi-for-coding`; smoke dispatch returned 'pong', cost 0). agent_policy omit-general: do not pass `--agent` at the top level. format_mode json: dispatch via `opencode run --format json` for the normalized token/cost/latency envelope. default_variant high + variant_status accepted-unverified: `--variant high` is accepted without error but its reasoning-effort effect is not benchmark-confirmed for this provider — safe to pass, do not depend on it. Subscription billing: dispatch cost shows 0. Re-verify slug before dispatch (Kimi/Moonshot ids drift).

strengths: dedicated Kimi For Coding subscription pool (`kimi-for-coding`), independent of minimax / xiaomi pools
coding-optimized Kimi K2.7 (display name 'Kimi K2.7 Code')
256k (262,144-token) context for long-file and multi-file work
subscription billing — dispatch cost 0 (no pay-per-token burn)

visual/illustration tasks: strongest brand restraint of the rotation — blue-led, orange reserved for one alert, gold rare, cohesive set mapping to feature facets (informal bento bake-off, n=1 fixture, 2026-06-22; see references/models/kimi-k2.7-code.md)
weaknesses: single executor path (no fallback if the kimi-for-coding pool is exhausted)
weaker framings (rcaf/cidi) occasionally miss adversarial edge cases on strict validators (benchmark 007) - use costar/race/tidd-ec
--variant effect unverified (flag accepted, reasoning-effort impact not benchmark-confirmed)

at --variant high on broad/large-repo scopes, over-explores (many sequential reads) and can exceed a 600s dispatch timeout WITHOUT emitting (opencode flushes only the final message -> 0 bytes; looks like a hang but is working); mitigate with a hard read-cap in the prompt + 1200s+ timeout, or omit --variant (observed 2026-06-17, n=few)

evidence.sample: 5-framework bakeoff on invalid-dominant STRICT VALIDATORS (validate-ipv4 + validate-date + validate-semver), 6 samples/cell, throttled serial real kimi-for-coding/k2p7 dispatches. (The 4th fixture hard-roman-to-int was excluded - its run stalled under orchestration churn; the 3-fixture result is conclusive.) Unlike the saturated run 006, correctness SEPARATED here. Per-framework correctness (n=18): tidd-ec 1.0 = race 1.0 = costar 1.0 (perfect tier) > cidi 0.996 > rcaf 0.992. Trust verdict: TIE on correctness (the 3 perfect frameworks cannot be statistically separated, top-pair margin 0, 90% CI [0,0]). The actionable signal: rcaf (the former convention default) is objectively the WEAKEST - it produced strict-validator code that missed adversarial cases - so it is retired/avoided for this model. Among the perfect tier, costar is primary for cross-evidence robustness (also MiMo's empirical winner, benchmark 004; favored by run 006's judge); tidd-ec is the most token-efficient (25 vs costar 70 median words) and is the fallback. This objectively refutes run 006's subjective gpt-5.5 judge, which had wrongly ranked rcaf/race highest.

executors.notes: Default MiniMax path. MiniMax Token Plan (subscription) via opencode provider `minimax-coding-plan`; Anthropic-compatible endpoint `https://api.minimax.io/anthropic/v1` (China: api.minimaxi.com). Live slug `minimax-coding-plan/MiniMax-M3` (plain); on opencode 1.16.2 `opencode models minimax-coding-plan` lists M2/M2.1/M2.5/M2.5-highspeed/M2.7/M2.7-highspeed/M3 — there is NO `MiniMax-M3-highspeed` (verified 2026-06-06). Omit `--agent` (rejected on opencode 1.15.13). Best prompt framework: TIDD-EC + dense pre-planning (benchmark 003). --variant omitted by default.
Pay-per-token Direct API. Confirm the live id with `opencode models minimax` before dispatch. Omit `--agent`.

capability.notes: Plain `minimax-coding-plan/MiniMax-M3` confirmed live (2026-06-02; re-verified on opencode 1.16.2 2026-06-06); there is NO `MiniMax-M3-highspeed` on 1.16.2. agent_policy omit-general: `--agent` is rejected on opencode 1.15.13. format_mode json: dispatch via `opencode run --format json` for the normalized token/cost/latency envelope. default_variant high is the dispatch-layer default; `--variant` forwarding for this provider was historically omitted (unverified) — confirm acceptance before relying on a non-default variant. Direct API path: provider `minimax`, quota_pool `minimax-api`.

strengths: subscription billing (no pay-per-token burn) via the MiniMax Token Plan
separate quota pool (minimax-token-plan) independent of cognition and Xiaomi pools
responds well to guardrail-heavy framing (TIDD-EC) + dense pre-planning
Direct API alternative available via provider `minimax` (pay-per-token)
weaknesses: no MiniMax-M3-highspeed slug on opencode 1.16.2 (verified 2026-06-06) — dispatch plain MiniMax-M3
subscription request budget resets on a 5-hour rolling window
--variant behavior unverified (omitted by default)

evidence.sample: 7-fixture rig, real MiniMax dispatches (benchmark run on M2.7; contract carried to M3)

executors.notes: Xiaomi Token Plan (Europe) via opencode provider xiaomi-token-plan-ams (credential 'Xiaomi Token Plan (Europe)'). Live slug xiaomi-token-plan-ams/mimo-v2.5-pro (confirmed responsive on opencode 1.15.13, 2026-06-01); verify the full live list with 'opencode models xiaomi-token-plan-ams'. Omit --agent (warns + falls back on 1.15.13). Manual direct-provider reroute uses xiaomi/mimo-v2.5-pro when configured; no automatic gateway fallback is defined. Context window 1,000,000 tokens (released 2026-04-22). OpenAI-compatible endpoint https://token-plan-ams.xiaomimimo.com/v1; opencode cost shows 0/0 (Token-Plan subscription). --variant maps to MiMo reasoning effort (low/medium/high) in opencode settings — always dispatch with --variant high (confirmed accepted on opencode 1.15.13). Best prompt framework (benchmark 004, 10/10 real runs): COSTAR or RACE + lean pre-planning — frame for format/brevity, not guardrails; TIDD-EC/dense ranked last; avoid CIDI (intermittent tool-only file-writes).

capability.notes: Live slug `xiaomi-token-plan-ams/mimo-v2.5-pro` confirmed responsive on opencode 1.15.13 (2026-06-01). default_variant high: `--variant` maps to MiMo reasoning effort (low/medium/high) and `--variant high` is confirmed accepted — always dispatch with it. agent_policy omit-general: `--agent` warns and falls back on 1.15.13. format_mode json: OpenAI-compatible endpoint; dispatch via `opencode run --format json` for the normalized envelope. Manual direct-provider reroutes use `xiaomi/mimo-v2.5-pro` when configured; no automatic gateway fallback is defined.

strengths: 1,000,000-token context window (largest in the small-model rotation)
strongly agentic — sustains 1000+ sequential tool calls (SWE-bench Pro 57.2)
token-efficient — 40-60% fewer tokens/trajectory vs frontier models (Opus 4.6 / GPT-5.4)
Xiaomi Token Plan (Europe) subscription billing via provider xiaomi-token-plan-ams
separate quota pool (xiaomi-token-plan) independent of minimax / cognition pools
manual direct-provider reroute available at xiaomi/mimo-v2.5-pro when configured
weaknesses: long-context quality degrades past ~512k despite the 1M window
Token-Plan subscription request-window semantics unverified
prompt-framework benchmark 004 used 2 fixtures / single-sample — COSTAR vs RACE tie is within noise

visual/illustration tasks: over-accents — spreads orange/gold across most cards, weak color restraint despite clean shapes and a reserved-accent brief (informal bento bake-off, n=1 fixture, 2026-06-22; see references/models/mimo-v2.5-pro.md)

executors.notes: Z.AI GLM Coding Plan (subscription) via opencode provider `zai-coding-plan` (auth credential 'Z.AI Coding Plan'). Live slug `zai-coding-plan/glm-5.2`; confirmed present via `opencode models zai-coding-plan` 2026-06-28 → glm-4.5-air/glm-4.7/glm-5-turbo/glm-5.1/glm-5.2/glm-5v-turbo. Flagship long-horizon coding model (supersedes the gateway-only glm-5.1 removed in cli-opencode v1.3.15.0). context_length 1,000,000 / 131,072 output per Z.AI docs — opencode `models` did not expose limits, re-verify. Omit `--agent` at top level. GLM has native `reasoning_effort` (high/max); whether opencode `--variant` forwards to it is unverified — smoke-test before relying on it. Thinking is on by default and the Coding Plan endpoint preserves thinking. Re-verify the live slug before automation — Z.AI ids drift (provider also serves glm-4.7 / glm-5.1 / glm-5-turbo).

capability.notes: Live slug `zai-coding-plan/glm-5.2` confirmed present 2026-06-28 (`opencode models zai-coding-plan`). agent_policy omit-general: do not pass `--agent` at the top level. format_mode json: dispatch via `opencode run --format json` for the normalized token/cost/latency envelope. default_variant high + variant_status accepted-unverified: GLM's native `reasoning_effort` has high/max, but whether opencode `--variant high/max` forwards to it is not confirmed — smoke-test, do not depend on it. Subscription billing: confirm dispatch cost via smoke test. GLM dispatch quirks (Z.AI docs): thinking on by default + preserved thinking on the Coding Plan endpoint; function-calling `tool_choice` only `auto`; streaming tool calls need `tool_stream=true`. Re-verify slug before dispatch (Z.AI ids drift).

strengths: dedicated Z.AI GLM Coding Plan subscription pool (`zai-coding-plan`), independent of kimi / minimax / xiaomi pools
flagship long-horizon coding model (Z.AI docs: Terminal-Bench 2.1 81.0, SWE-bench Pro 62.1 — both above GLM-5.1)
1,000,000-token context for long-file and multi-repo work (128K max output)
subscription billing via the Z.AI Coding Plan
native reasoning_effort (high/max) + thinking-by-default for complex multi-constraint coding
weaknesses: single executor path (no fallback if the zai-coding-plan pool is exhausted)
context_length / max-output recorded from Z.AI docs (1,000,000 / 131,072) — opencode `models` did not expose limits; re-verify on the install
--variant ↔ reasoning_effort mapping unverified (GLM has native high/max; opencode forwarding not confirmed)

best prompt framework is a TIE among the perfect tier (costar=tidd-ec=cidi=race at correctness 1.0); costar is best-of-tied (tersest + cross-model corroborated), not a decisive single winner — benchmark 008

thinking-on-by-default drives high latency variance — 6-161s observed over benchmark 008's 45 small code-gen cells (avg ~26s, ~27x spread); budget generous timeouts, a tight timeout kills the slow tail

~1/45 dispatches was a transient infra failure (exit -1, dispatch_failed) in benchmark 008 — expect occasional transient dispatch failures at scale; retry the cell rather than treating it as a model error

evidence.sample: 5-framework bakeoff on invalid-dominant STRICT VALIDATORS (validate-ipv4 + validate-date + validate-semver), 3 samples/cell, real zai-coding-plan/glm-5.2 dispatches graded by the deterministic hidden-test oracle (grader noop, no LLM-judge bias). Run status SEPARABLE: per-framework correctness costar 1.0 = tidd-ec 1.0 = cidi 1.0 = race 1.0 (perfect tier) > rcaf 0.976 (the ONLY sub-perfect framework; also worst format adherence 0.889). Trust verdict TIE on correctness among the perfect tier (top-pair costar vs tidd-ec, margin 0, 90% CI [0,0]). costar is primary as best-of-tied: perfect correctness + tersest output (13 median words vs tidd-ec 38, cidi/race 94) + cross-model corroboration (also the empirical winner for kimi benchmark 007 and mimo benchmark 004). tidd-ec fallback (perfect, 2nd-tersest). rcaf avoided (only correctness misses + worst format). NOTE: CRAFT (the phase-1 doc-guided default) is NOT in the harness framework registry [rcaf,race,cidi,tidd-ec,costar] so it could not be benchmarked; the empirical winner among the 5 supported frameworks replaces it. 1/45 dispatches was a transient infra failure (exit -1, excluded from scoring).

executors.notes: Future small-model fallback candidate. Adoption blocked until quota-pool semantics verified.

strengths: separate quota pool
fast natural-language synthesis
future fallback candidate
weaknesses: not in active rotation
unverified for this workflow
requires adoption before routing
